name: Enterprise SLA Breach Prevention
trigger: Active probe latency on enterprise slice exceeds 80% of contracted SLA threshold for 15 continuous minutes.

autonomyLevels.L1: NOC engineer alerted. Manual resource reallocation within 30 minutes.
autonomyLevels.L2: Automated QoS adjustment within slice parameters. Engineer notified.
autonomyLevels.L3: Cross-domain resource reallocation. SLA clock paused pending resolution. Customer portal updated.
autonomyLevels.L4: Network autonomously reallocates resources, prevents breach, and logs compliance evidence — before SLA clock would have triggered.

economicOutcome: SLA breach penalty prevented. Enterprise contract renewal protected.
economicCalculation: Typical enterprise SLA breach penalty: 10–20% monthly contract value. For a €50K/month enterprise contract: €5K–€10K per breach. At 12 potential breaches/year: €60K–€120K penalty avoidance.
combinationUpgrade: Add passive probe (RADCOM ACE) to identify which enterprise users were affected within the slice — enabling precise SLA evidence and impact scoping.
